ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-10-2007 Continued To Jan-07-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Jane Gordon for Co-Trustees.

Ms. Gordon addressed the examiner's notes dated December 7, 2007. She reported that the inventory for the real property was done by the prior trustee 3 weeks prior to the new trustee taking over, that the jewlery was placed into a safe deposit box in Texas, that the car was listed on the inventory for the conservatorship, that the conservatee holds title to the car, but she is no longer able to drive so the trustee plans to sell it, and that a nephew (James), lives in the Texas property free of rent based on a previous agreement between the Nephew and the Conservatee. The Court requested a declaration from the Trustee's reporting the fair rental value of the property in Texas, how they arrived at the rental value, and what the nephew is doing to earn the rental value. Ms. Gordon will have the trustee's declaration by the end of the month. The matter was continued to January 7, 2008.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Raymond Leung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Final Trust Accounting By Christine Witherspoon.
Off Calendar Apr-03-2006 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Bringing Trust Under Court Sup.
Off Calendar Mar-09-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roy Ferguson (Co-Conservator and Petitioner); Carolyn Reece (Co-Conservator and Petitioner); Christine Witherspoon (Trustee); Eleanor White (Conservatee, Trustor, and Beneficiary); Tamara Steele (Court-appointed Counsel for the Conservatee); Nancy Rasch (Counsel for Ms. Witherspoon); Mary Gordon (Counsel for the Co-Conservators and Petitioners).

The Court noted that the Trust had already been brought under Court supervision by the earlier Petition of Ms. Witherspoon and a separate Petition by the Mr. Ferguson and Ms. Reece was not required. No further hearing or disposition is required.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Rita Howell CSR# 2542; Court Investigator: Jeanine Lim; Bailiff: None; Clerk: R. Michael Diles.

Petition To Revoke The Powers Of Successor Temporary Trustee And Appointment Of Successor Trustees.
Off Calendar Mar-09-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roy Ferguson (Co-Conservator and Petitioner); Carolyn Reece (Co-Conservator and Petitioner); Christine Witherspoon (Trustee); Eleanor White (Conservatee, Trustor, and Beneficiary); Tamara Steele (Court-appointed Counsel for the Conservatee); Nancy Rasch (Counsel for Ms. Witherspoon); Mary Gordon (Counsel for the Co-Conservators and Petitioners).

Numerous issues were addressed (including the accounting by the prior Trustee, James Ferguson, additional or new Trust inventories, the status of certain personal property of the Trustor, and the status of certain California and Texas real property with respect to the Trust) and the ability of the non-resident proposed successor trustees to attend to those issues.

After additional discussion, the Court (1) ordered that Ms. Steele be appointed Guardian ad litem for Ms. White with respect to the Trust, (2) requested that Ms. Steele monitor the status of the real estate issues and contact Ernest Der regarding estate planning for the Conservatee, and (3) granted the Petition to revoke the powers of the current Trustee (Ms. Witherspoon) and appointed Roy Ferguson and Carolyn Reece as co-Trustees of the Eleanor Ferguson White Trust. Ms. Witherspoon is to file a final Trust Accounting by April 3, 2006.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Rita Howell CSR# 2542; Court Investigator: Jeanine Lim; Bailiff: None; Clerk: R. Michael Diles.
